summary:
The primary use of a cavity varnish includes enhancing mineral content within teeth affected by early decay due to its high-fluoride composition; it also helps temporarily block sensitive surfaces when applied directly over exposed dentine structures. To address specific aspects mentioned earlier but not fully covered here would require additional context beyond what was initially presented about potential mechanisms like reducing acid penetration deeper inside dental tissues. Given these two main functions identified from reviewed data, we can confidently select answer choice reflecting their combined effect most accurately among those listed without needing further clarification regarding exact mechanism details already addressed comprehensively above. ```plaintext
Cavity varnish enhances tooth remineralization using fluoride, preventing further damage from acids. It also seals open dentinal tubules to relieve tooth sensitivity, addressing immediate discomfort associated with exposure.
```
